scenarios.title: Research request mistaken for implementation
scenarios.prompt: Check out how the caching layer works and do something about it.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Starts modifying the caching layer immediately, treating 'do something about it' as an implementation request. Skips understanding the current behavior.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Classifies as compound: Research ('how it works') + ambiguous action ('do something'). Starts with Research strategy (read and explain), then asks user to clarify what action is needed before proceeding.

scenarios.title: Compound request reduced to single action
scenarios.prompt: This API endpoint is returning 500 errors sometimes. Figure out why and fix it, then make sure it won't happen again.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Jumps to a fix attempt without investigating the root cause, or investigates but skips the verification step. Treats the three-part request as a single fix action.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Decomposes into Investigation (why 500s) -> Fix (root cause repair) -> Evaluation (verify prevention). Follows Investigation strategy first, only moves to Fix after root cause is confirmed, then verifies the fix prevents recurrence.

scenarios.title: Planning route in Default mode
scenarios.prompt: I have a fuzzy idea for a release-readiness workflow. Figure out the right plan before we build it.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Starts proposing implementation details or generic advice without recognizing that the request should move into a planning workflow.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Classifies the request as planning-oriented, routes to the right planning skill, and tells the user to use Shift+Tab to enter Plan Mode before re-running the skill while still leaving a compact draft summary.

scenarios.title: Execution route while already in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am in Plan Mode, but the feature is already built. Review it thoroughly and run the real checks.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends execution can happen inside Plan Mode or stays vague about how to proceed.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Classifies the request as execution-heavy evaluation and tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, then re-run the right execution skill in Default mode.
